context: 1985 was turbulent in apartheid South Africa, with states of emergency declared. Music became a vehicle for resistance and escape. Bubblegum pop — a synth-driven township style — exploded with Yvonne Chaka Chaka and Brenda Fassie. Anti-apartheid anthems coexisted with dance music, and Johnny Clegg bridged racial divides through his collaborations.

tracks.title: I'm in Love with a DJ
